text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 02 October 2025

Dr Alex Morgan
Riverside Health Centre

12 Mill Lane, Southwark, London SE1 3AB
Dear Dr Alex Morgan
Jordan Ellis, DOB: 14-02-1991, Hospital No: H927461 NHS No. 9021457763
Flat 3, 28 Willow Street, London EC2A 4BH

Diagnoses/summary: Combined generalised and focal epilepsy, with both generalised and focal seizure types documented; prior EEGs on record and previous assessments in secondary care. Personalised self-management plan is documented elsewhere and has been shared with the patient.

Neuroimaging: MRI brain (2018) reported structurally normal study; no clear epileptogenic lesion identified.

Previous and current anti-seizure medications: Previously tried valproate (effective for generalised events but weight gain), carbamazepine (helped focal spells but mood low), levetiracetam (behavioural change), lamotrigine (partial benefit), and clobazam PRN for clusters. Currently on lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily and brivaracetam 100 mg twice daily. Rescue: intranasal midazolam held by partner for prolonged events.

Comorbidities and relevant history: Mild asthma; no known cardiac disease. Sleep restriction is a prominent trigger. Reports occasional cluster patterns temporally associated with nights of curtailed sleep. Does not drive.

Clinic review: I saw Jordan Ellis in clinic today, accompanied by a partner. They report two distinct seizure phenotypes. The first is a focal onset impaired awareness episode with a rising epigastric sensation, brief behavioural arrest and oral automatisms, usually lasting under two minutes, followed by fatigue. The second phenotype is a generalised tonic-clonic seizure occurring typically out of sleep, particularly after disrupted nights. Frequency over the past three months has been approximately one focal event every 2–3 weeks, with three generalised tonic-clonic seizures in that period. They note occasional clustering of focal events after sleep loss; this is captured in their personalised plan.

EEG summary: Background shows intermittent epileptiform activity between seizures without captured clinical events; no clear electrographic correlate of habitual attacks recorded during the most recent study. This fits with the clinical picture of both generalised and focal epilepsy types.

Lifestyle and triggers: They report improved seizure control when sleep is regular. Alcohol minimal. They use a smartphone reminder system aligned with the personalised plan and have shared it with family members.

Plan:
- Continue lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily and brivaracetam 100 mg twice daily.
- Maintain rescue intranasal midazolam as per existing protocol in the personalised plan.
- Reinforce sleep hygiene measures given the temporal association between sleep loss and clustering. Patient-led strategies already in place and documented elsewhere.
- Arrange ambulatory EEG over 48 hours to sample usual sleep and wake patterns; aim to better characterise interictal patterns and any brief events not perceived clinically.
- Routine bloods (FBC, U&E, LFTs) and lamotrigine level for reference only, given recent dose stability.
- Provide updated seizure diary templates; patient prefers digital logging.
- Follow-up in 4 months, earlier if clusters increase or generalised events recur despite good sleep.

I have reiterated SUDEP risk factors and safety advice around bathing, heights and sleeping position. The patient confirms understanding and has a contingency plan with their partner, consistent with the personalised plan held on record.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: approximately one focal event every 2–3 weeks

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter states two seizure types with frequencies over the past three months: focal events at approximately one every 2–3 weeks, and three generalised tonic-clonic seizures over three months (≈1 per month). Per rule 7, select the highest frequency among multiple types. 1 per 2–3 weeks is more frequent than 1 per month. Clustering is mentioned but not quantified, so it cannot be used in the cluster format.
seizure_frequency_number: approximately one focal event every 2–3 weeks
reference: Interictal discharges on EEG; no ictal correlate

flaws.description: Gold (1 per 2 to 3 weeks) disagrees with Reference (unknown)
